independent_reconstruction_of_the_mechan.summary: Beneficial Genesis (as reconstructed pre-freeze from design materials and issues #33/#34) is a finite-epoch genesis allocation admission mechanism, not ongoing consensus. A participant presents a claim that a synthetic Bitcoin-like transaction (1) shows source-chain control (synthetic HMAC stand-in), (2) pays exact genesis-committed charity scriptPubKey bytes (not address strings), and (3) binds a post-quantum destination key via an OP_RETURN commitment carrier 6a20||32-byte digest. After epoch close, a fixed integer genesis pool is allocated proportionally to eligible satoshis with floor division; remainder is unissued. Nullifiers prevent double claims of the same (source_chain, txid, vout).
lifecycle: Genesis: commit charity set, fixed pool, epoch id, min confirmations, optional quantum cutoff
Open epoch: admit claims under fail-closed validation predicate; consume nullifiers
Close: freeze accepted tip height/hash and last-eligible inclusion height/hash; closed=true
Allocate: proportional floor over admitted eligible sats; publish GenesisAllocationRecord
validation_predicate_expected: confirmations >= min from tip; inclusion height <= last_eligible
exact charity script matches active genesis entry
commitment binds chain/epoch/charity/vout/amount/version/nonce/PQ key; present in same tx
PQ stand-in signature verifies under bound key and fixed domain
no new admission after epoch close
optional reject classical source auth after quantum_compromise_cutoff_height
allocation arithmetic integer-only and supply-conserving when closed

allocation_rule.ordering: claim rows ordered by nullifier_hex ascending
allocation_rule.no_exchange_rate: eligible sats are weights only; no fixed BTC-to-token rate

encoding_rules.canonical_json: UTF-8, sorted keys, separators (',', ':'), ensure_ascii=True, no NaN

independent_reconstruction_of_the_mechan.economics_track_from_issue_34_expected_n: Issue #34 defines a separate adversarial economics red-team with deterministic simulators, whale/rebate/Sybil/governance/tainted-fund scenarios, and a recommendation among REJECT_OR_REDESIGN / CONTINUE_WITH_CONDITIONS / ECONOMIC_GATE_PASS. Implementation packages exist on disk (SHA inventoried) but content is unread until post-freeze.

expected_load_bearing_claims.claim: Design pack reference verifier admits only claims satisfying the published fail-closed predicate on synthetic fixtures.
Allocation uses integer-only floor proportional rule with unissued remainder; total_issued + remainder = pool and total_issued <= pool.
Nullifiers are domain-separated domain_hash(DOMAIN_NULLIFIER, source_chain, txid32, u32be(vout)) and prevent double-claim of same output.
Commitment carrier is exact 34-byte script 6a20||commitment; address-string comparison is forbidden.
Evidence gate regenerates fixtures and executes all executable invalid vectors; documentary scenarios are not counted as verifier passes.
Cryptography is synthetic stand-in; pack does not claim real PQ or real Bitcoin script/consensus.
Program issue #33 positions Beneficial Genesis as primary research focus without promoting STATUS.json or authorizing live funds.
An economics red-team package and subsequent Breaker/retest packages exist under inventoried paths and claim to answer issue #34's mechanism-safety question with deterministic simulations.
expected_load_bearing_claims.source_surface: path existence + SHA inventory only; content unread

expected_load_bearing_claims.claim: A second-implementation differential retest package exists (BENEFICIAL_GENESIS_RETEST_002) relative to the design verifier.
expected_load_bearing_claims.source_surface: path existence + SHA inventory only; content unread

assumptions_requiring_evidence: Committed design fixtures match regenerate_fixtures output byte-for-byte.
Verifier implementation matches TECHNICAL_DESIGN.md (not only tests).
Bools are rejected as integers at all type boundaries (allocation observed; full verifier unread).
Duplicate JSON keys and unknown fields are rejected at parser/object boundaries.
Economics simulators are deterministic under published seeds and conserve allocation invariants.
Breaker and retest packages independently reproduce or honestly disagree with red-team claims.
Receipts bind to exact commits and write scopes without unauthorized mutations.
Clean-environment reproduction succeeds with only documented prerequisites.
No load-bearing claim confuses synthetic pass with production Bitcoin/PQ readiness.
Cross-model economics comparison is not mere shared-premise circularity.
plausible_failure_modes: Spec/code drift: verifier admits vectors design text forbids, or rejects valid design cases.
Parser weakness: duplicate keys, unknown fields, Unicode/NUL, malformed hex, bool-as-int accepted.
Nullifier domain omission or cross-chain/epoch collision accepted.
Commitment multiplicity ambiguity or carrier substring acceptance.
Reorg leaves stale nullifier consumed after tip switch.
Allocation remainder gaming issues more than pool under alternate reimplementation.
Economics metrics use wrong denominators or non-deterministic lottery without fixed seeds.
Tests encode assumptions rather than properties; fixtures generate expected values circularly.
Receipts overstate independence, clean-room status, or pass/fail chronology.
Composition mismatch: design allocation vs economics models disagree on same inputs.
Environmental failures misclassified as product failures (or vice versa).

falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-DES-01: Any executable valid fixture rejected, or executable invalid fixture admitted with wrong/missing rejection code under clean reproduction.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-DES-02: Construct eligible set where sum(floor allocations) > pool, or remainder_unissued + total_issued != pool, or float/bool accepted.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-DES-03: Two successful admits for same (source_chain,txid,vout), or domain-omitted nullifier accepted.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-DES-04: Claim admitted based on address string without exact script match, or non-6a20 carrier accepted for commitment.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-DES-05: verify_evidence.py exit 0 while fixture bytes diverge from generator or executable invalid vector skipped.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-DES-06: Any package claim of production PQ/Bitcoin security without replacement of stand-ins.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-PROG-01: STATUS.json modified by Beneficial Genesis packages or live funds authorized in artifacts.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-ECON-01: Simulator non-reproducible, missing required scenarios from issue #34, or recommendation unbacked by machine-readable results.
falsifiers_for_each_major_claim.CLAIM-DIFF-01: Second implementation systematically disagrees with reference on fixtures without documented minimized counterexamples, or cleanroom claim contradicted by provenance.

clean_environment_reproduction: Claude economics simulator + tests (exact commands from package README post-freeze)
independent_hostile_probes: duplicate JSON keys at raw boundary
bool-as-int / float at allocation and object parsers
Unicode and embedded NUL in string fields
nullifier domain omission and cross-domain variants
allocation extreme values and multi-claim remainder
commitment multiplicity and wrong carrier forms
economics public-function validators: negatives, zero, duplicates, bps, lottery
composition: design allocate_proportional vs economics pro-rata on shared numeric cases
provenance_checks: receipt commit binding vs git log ancestry
no subject path mutation after simulator runs

freeze_independence_statement: This PRE_AUDIT_FREEZE records independent reconstruction and planned tests before opening economics implementations, prior Breaker/retest packages, or PR #35/#37/#39. A freeze reduces anchoring to those later materials but does not prove perfect independence: the auditor has already seen design-pack documentation, partial protocol code (constants.py, allocation.py), STATUS.json, safety instructions, and exact issues #33 and #34; path names and SHA-256 inventories of unread packages were computed; the controlling task prompt itself describes expected package structure and audit dimensions. Residual anchoring risk remains. Permanently forbidden sibling 2x2 audit materials were not opened.
non_actions: do not assign or imply an R-round
do not alter existing Beneficial Genesis subject packages
do not use live funds, select real charities, solicit donations, issue a token, or deploy
